behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff: CSV parser/row index -> schema validator -> DLQ writer payload. A unit test of only validator logic can miss row-number propagation bugs (e.g., off-by-one after header handling) when wiring modules. Integration-brake check: (a) no explicit OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text, so System is not required; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires rejected rows to appear in DLQ with row number, not real network/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process pipeline slice (parser + validator + DLQ adapter) on a CSV fixture with missing required fields; assert each invalid row is sent to DLQ with the exact original CSV row number and valid rows are not sent.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure transformation correctness at one function/object boundary (date parsing/normalization), with no required I/O. MOCK-MISS says Unit is sufficient because the described failure is parsing/algorithmic, not inter-module handoff. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration and above because behavior text is only about converting date strings to canonical format, with no DB/network/subprocess/LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the date-normalization function directly with representative ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including ambiguous and invalid examples) and assert canonical output or explicit validation error.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is runtime resource behavior: memory pressure and timeout under large-load execution. In-process mocks/fakes hide allocator/GC/process-memory effects and true wall-clock throughput. REAL-INFRA applies (OS/process behavior), so System is the minimum. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires 'within the configured timeout' and 'without running out of memory,' which are real runtime characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process on a 500k-row fixture with production-like config; measure completion before timeout and verify no OOM/kill occurred, with peak memory below limit.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is retry-policy orchestration between pipeline ingest logic and S3 client abstraction. This can be caught with module wiring plus a deterministic S3 test double returning 503 then success; no model output involved. Integration-brake check: (a) not required to validate real network stack behavior from text, only retry/backoff decision logic; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text targets retry/backoff behavior on transient 503, not real socket-level/network-condition correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire ingest service to an S3 stub that returns 503 for first N calls then success; use controllable clock/scheduler to assert exponential backoff intervals and that batch eventually succeeds instead of hard-failing.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes restart semantics plus real PostgreSQL write/idempotency behavior across process lifecycle. MOCK-HIDE risk is high: mocked DB or in-memory repo can miss duplicate writes caused by transaction/checkpoint ordering under actual restart. REAL-INFRA applies due restart as subprocess/OS event and real DB semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against real PostgreSQL, interrupt process mid-batch after partial commits, restart with same input/checkpoint state, then assert no duplicate rows (by unique key/count invariants) and full batch completeness.
